name: FreshBooks API
what_it_does: Cloud invoicing and accounting software API for freelancers and small businesses. Strong focus on time tracking, project-based billing, invoicing, and expense management.
use_cases: Automating invoice generation from tracked time and project data
Pulling client and project data into agent-driven reporting workflows
Syncing expense records between FreshBooks and other business tools
Automating payment reminders and collection workflows
Building client-facing billing portals integrated with FreshBooks data
not_for: Full double-entry accounting (limited accounting depth vs QuickBooks/Xero)
Enterprise businesses with complex financial requirements
best_when: An agent automates invoicing, time tracking, or billing workflows for service-based freelancers or small agencies already using FreshBooks.
avoid_when: You need full accounting depth, inventory, or are serving enterprise customers.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow with PKCE support. Access tokens expire in 1 hour, refresh tokens are long-lived. Bearer token in Authorization header. Application must be registered in FreshBooks developer portal.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Trial period available; no permanent free tier
pricing.notes: API access included with any FreshBooks subscription. No separate API pricing. Developer sandbox available for testing. 30-day free trial.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns HTTP errors with JSON bodies. Error codes are numeric with text messages. Documentation quality is inconsistent — some endpoints are better documented than others. Community resources are limited.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Duplicate invoice creation is possible on retry. Agents must check existence before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Account ID (business account ID) is required in almost all API paths — agents must retrieve it first
OAuth2 required — no simpler API key option for agent access
FreshBooks uses both account_id and identity_id which are different things — easy to confuse
API versioning via URL path (/api/v3) but endpoint availability varies by account tier
Webhook payload format differs from REST API response format — requires separate parsing logic
Rate limits not publicly documented — need to handle 429 defensively

security_score_components.security_notes: Accounting/invoicing platform. OAuth2. Financial data — sensitive. Invoice and client data is PII. Restrict agent access to required financial operations only.
